TextENGNew: "After them! Don't let them escape!"
The gruff shouts of police officers echoed from the garden.
The back room next door had fallen silent.
When I gently slid open the fusuma, I found Miyuki-chan on the other side, cuffing the men in black one after another.
Detectives sprinted after several of the black-suited men who'd fled toward the garden.
"Phew... I'll leave the rest to you."
Miyuki-chan called out to the officers beside her, and the handcuffed men were passed off to other detectives and led out of the back room.
...Before I even realized it, everything was over.
"Miyuki-chan..."
"Ryuunosuke-san... are you hurt?"
"Oh no, blood...?!"
Miyuki-chan rushed over to check on me.
She wiped the blood from the tip of my nose with her fingertips, then cupped my cheeks in both hands and peered intently into my face.
So much had happened since I first met Miyuki-chan yesterday morning, but... looking at her now, right here in front of me, she was still as cute as ever.
"I'm sorry for putting you through all of this, Ryuunosuke-san.@k@vS10/40/410300971. I wanted to get here sooner, but..."

"It's all right.@k@vS10/62/410101689. I was totally fine.@k@vS10/62/410101690. ...More importantly, I'm sorry about all this.@k@vS10/62/410101691. I wish we could've straightened everything out before you got here, Miyuki-chan..."
"What are you talking about?!@k@vS10/40/410300973. Isn't this exactly the sort of situation you should rely on the power of the state for?!"
Miyuki-chan flashed a friendly smile.
Her smiling face seemed to soothe all my pain and weariness.
All the muscles in my body loosened up, and I felt comfortable enough that I wouldn't even regret it if I died here.
I wasn't sure if my feelings were able to get through to her, but Miyuki-chan looked back at me with a huge smile.
"Hey... Miyuki-chan...@k@vS10/62/410101693. About... that promise..."
"Huh?"
"No, I mean..."
I continued to stammer as Miyuki-chan gazed at me.
She looked like she was just waiting for the words to come out... but I suddenly started to feel embarrassed and turned away.
...What am I doing acting like some naive middle school boy?! Don't forget how old you are!!
Well, that's all right. ...At any rate... being good friends seems like a nice place to start...
"Umm... anyway..."
"Ryuunosuke-san, what's wrong...?@k@vS10/40/410300976. Are you all right...?"
Miyuki-chan held my hand tight while firmly gazing into my eyes.
Could it be...? Is that handhold an invitation?!
My heart was beating far more intensely than usual.
But it was a totally different sort of tension from the gun battle earlier. It felt good.
"Miyuki-chan..."
"Ryuunosuke-san..."
With Miyuki-chan gazing up at me with a slight blush in her cheeks, my heart started beating so violently it felt like it was going to burst out of my body.
"Miyuki-chan..."
Come on, now's your chance!
I pulled Miyuki-chan's hand in closer, and with determination...
"...What are you doing with my daughter?"
"Aaah?!"
I heard a gruff voice coming from behind, and quickly let go of Miyuki-chan's hand.
When I turned in that direction, a tall, slender man dressed in a suit put a hand on Miyuki-chan's shoulder and stared at me over his shoulder.
"Hello there.@k@vS10/13/410900003. I'm her father."
...His frightening eyes were glaring straight at me, causing me to freeze up for a totally different reason this time.
In other words, it was... hair-raising.
"Aren't you... Akasaka-san?!"
I remember now. ...I'm certain that was the detective from the Metropolitan police department that I met while investigating the Hinamizawa tapes my father left behind.
Akasaka-san seems to have changed a bit since I first met him. It's like he was more dignified, solemn, awe-inspiring, and refined...
But... what was Akasaka-san doing in Hinamizawa again...?
"Huh...?@k@vS10/13/410900005. Might you be... Arakawa-kun?"
"Y-yes, that's right."
"Oh, it's you?@k@vS10/13/410900007. And here I thought you were some daredevil making a pass on my daughter.@k@vS10/13/410900008. I'm sorry, forgive my rudeness."
"Hah, haha..."
Akasaka-san seemed to grin broadly as soon as he recognized me.
Umm... Akasaka-san. You may say that, but could you suppress that bloodlust that could kill any insect that got too close to you...?
"What? Dad?!@k@vS10/40/410300980. You know Ryuunosuke-san?!"
"You could say that.@k@vS10/13/410900010. ...Hmm? Ryuunosuke...?"
"Oh, about that..."
I desperately tried to evade his questioning. Please, let me out of here.
Miyuki-chan's eyes were open wide in a daze as she watched all this.

"Well, I've visited the neighboring town of Okinomiya several times while investigating the Great Hinamizawa Disaster.@k@vS10/13/410900012. I met Arakawa-kun a few times to discuss some old Hinamizawa-related tapes he brought with him."
"Is that so...?"
"Seems like you've been doing investigating of your own, just like your father.@k@vS10/13/410900014. But even so, try not to work yourself too hard."
"Haah... haha. Okay."
I tried to give a suitable retort.
But the truth is... my father actually met Akasaka-san, too.

When my father interviewed the survivor of the Great Hinamizawa Disaster who predicted he would "drown to death," he started to grow interested in the disaster itself... and during that investigation, the two of them got to know one another.
According to my father's investigation notes, Akasaka-san claimed that a resident of Hinamizawa predicted his wife's death...
So, meeting up with Akasaka-san, who my father met during his own investigation in Hinamizawa, felt like an act of fate.
"Your father was a splendid reporter.@k@vS10/13/410900016. ...But you can be even better than him."
"...I'm not like my father.@k@vS10/62/410101708. All I'm doing is walking through life trying not to embarrass myself."
Akasaka-san must have thought those comments were exceedingly pure for a man in his 30s like me.
That's why he bitterly smiled.
But there was a gentle warmth in his eyes.
"Haah... it's a small world.@k@vS10/40/410300983. Well, if I had connected to you via Dad instead of that publishing company, I probably would've met you sooner."
"...What do you mean, Miyuki-chan?@k@vS10/62/410101710. Who is this "Dad" you keep mentioning...?"
"Huh?@k@vS10/40/410300985. Ohh, this is my father."
"H-huuuuuh?!"
Akasaka-san... is Miyuki-chan's... father?
They're nothing alike. Not at all.
How could someone as cute as Miyuki-chan be born from someone like Akasaka-san? I can't believe it!
But... looking a little closer, Akasaka-san didn't look so bad, himself.
Huh...?
So, the two of them really are... father and daughter...?
Unbelievable. Impossible. Absolutely absurd!
"Arakawa-kun certainly is a fine young man, but...@k@vS10/13/410900018. wouldn't your husband be in tears if he knew you were running around with other men?"
"What are you saying?@k@vS10/40/410300987. Don't be stupid. Besides, don't you have things to take care of?"
"Hahahaha.@k@vS10/13/410900020. ...Alright, later then."
Akasaka-san let out a small smile and bowed to me, then walked off in the direction of Mifune's corpse.
No... more importantly.
Akasaka-san just said something reaaaally important just now, didn't he...?
"What...?@k@vS10/62/410101713. What was that... about your husband?"
"...Just my father being obnoxious."
"No, if you're Akasaka-san's daughter...@k@vS10/62/410101715. but... your last name is Sorimachi..."

"That's right.@k@vS10/40/410300990. I changed my name when I got married.@k@vS10/40/410300991. ...My father and I are both detectives.@k@vS10/40/410300992. And on top of that, my husband is a detective too.@k@vS10/40/410300993. ...We're a weird family."
"Wh-whaaaaaat?!"
Miyuki-chan lightly smiled as she said that.
I, on the other hand... couldn't laugh about it.
Miyuki-chan was married this whole time?!
My shock must've been plain to see. Miyuki-chan deeply lowered her head and said, "I'm sorry!" several times in a row.
"I don't have a boyfriend, but I do have a husband.@k@vS10/40/410300995. At first I was just playing a joke on you, but I never really had an opportunity to come clean about it..."
...Sounds like she was planning to drop the "Actually, I'm maaarried♪" on me at some point and have a good laugh at my expense.
But... up until now, we never really had a moment to relax...
The timing just wasn't appropriate to reveal it and laugh at the "Whaaat, really?" reaction...
"Um... Ryuunosuke-san, is there a chance that you...?"
As she said that,
Miyuki-chan looked terribly distraught, afraid to face me directly.
She wasn't trying to lead me around... but she seems terribly upset that she might've carelessly hurt me.
Well... I'd be lying if I said it didn't hurt...
Still, her intentions weren't malicious, so it was my duty as a man to make things easier on her. ...Probably.
"N-nooooo, nothing like that!"
"I'm just really surprised to learn that you're Akasaka-san's daughter, Miyuki-chan. It really is a small world.@k@vS10/62/410101719. So really, there's nothing for you to be worried about!"
"Really?"
"R-really!"
Miyuki-chan flashed me a big grin.
I... had no choice but to nod to her with a vague smile.
Aah, goodbye my innocent love.
Well, it's not like it really began in the first place.
"...Ah, this is bad.@k@vS10/40/410301001. It'll cause trouble if you guys stay here... so why don't you get going, Ryuunosuke-san?"
"Huh?"
"I've got a lot to do here, so I won't be able to head back today.@k@vS10/40/410301003. Leave everything to the police from here on out!"
Miyuki-chan pushed me by the back, trying to drive me away.
It certainly would be a bother to stick around in a place where police are conducting on-site investigations and interrogations.
At that point, I realized that Sonozaki-san and Otobe had long since disappeared.
"Alright... later, Miyuki-chan..."
"Ryuunosuke-san...@k@vS10/40/410301005. Thanks... for everything."
Miyuki-chan deeply bowed her head.
I lightly lowered my head in response, then headed out of the back room.
Once I reached the gate, I took one more look toward the back room... and saw Miyuki-chan standing on the side, smiling and waving to me as she watched me head out.
I was a little out of touch (or slow?) on the subject of romance, and I was led astray... but, yeah, she's a good girl.
I'll send her an email next time I have an opportunity.
I'd be proud to consider her a "friend" who endured this situation without falling into a bad relationship...
Several patrol cars were parked outside the gate.
So, which way did they go...?
For now, I decided to walk in the direction I came from.
Before long, I found Otobe and Sonozaki-san sitting on a big stone by the side of the road, waiting for me.
"Otobe...! Sonozaki-san...!"
I ran toward them.
Then, a familiar girl came walking this way from the other end of the street.
"Towada-san!@k@vS10/41/410201000. ...I'm glad you're safe!"
"You too, Otobe-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600677. The most important thing is that everyone is safe."
Otobe grabbed Towada-san's hand and gave her a huge smile to celebrate that they were both alive.
"...Oh, Sonozaki-san, let me return the gun you lent me.@k@vS10/42/410600679. Thank you very much."
"Oh... sure."
Towada-san handed the gun she borrowed from Mion-san over to Shion-san.
Shion-san seemed a bit confused, but she decided to leave it at that since a proper explanation would've taken far too long.
Then, once we realized that Towada-san came to see us off, we all turned toward her.
"...That's right.@k@vS10/41/410201002. Umm, Towada-san... what are you planning to do from here on?"
"...After everyone left, I faced Takumi-kun and came to a decision.@k@vS10/42/410600681. ...It's time to start over and live a new life."
"And, in order to make things right... I'm going to turn myself in."
"Oh, really...?"

"Same here... I think I'm ready to start a new life.@k@vS10/41/410201004. I'll find a way to repay my debts.@k@vS10/41/410201005. I'll talk things over with my parents properly.@k@vS10/41/410201006. If I search hard enough, I'm sure I'll be able to find a path forward."
"...It probably won't be easy... but I've decided. I'm not going to run away anymore."
...Life is like a tube of toothpaste.
...You have to squeeze every last drop out of it.
I thought back on what Mion-san said earlier.
I certainly agree with that assessment. Rather than hesitating, the best choice at any given time is to simply do whatever you can...
"Oh, Arakawa-san.@k@vS10/41/410201009. After I've cleaned everything up... would you consider hiring me as your assistant?"
"Don't be stupid. I'm not nearly that important."
"Huh?"
"I was lying about being a famous writer. It's not true.@k@vS10/62/410101728. I can hardly make the rent on my run-down apartment. I was even thinking of giving up the writing business."
"Seriously?!"
I scratched my head. ...His dramatic reaction was a little embarrassing.
"Well, I'm half-kidding.@k@vS10/62/410101730. But... if there's one thing I learned, it's that I've really come to appreciate Hinamizawa."
"The truth is... after everything that's happened, I've started to like it.@k@vS10/62/410101732. Regardless of everything else, I heard the voice of the dead for the first time ever!"
"...I was probably taking it too easy.@k@vS10/62/410101734. I just half-assed work, only caring if I was having fun, so I don't know what the future has in store for me."
But... I won't hesitate any longer.
It seems like... I finally managed to find my way.

"I've also come to a conclusion.@k@vS10/62/410101736. I'm certain that the spirits of the dead persist even when the body is destroyed.@k@vS10/62/410101737. ...And sometimes, they need to borrow the body of a human to carry out their will."

"I'm glad I met all of you.@k@vS10/62/410101739. ...And I think I'll keep going with this line of work.@k@vS10/62/410101740. Some day, I really will be able to say I'm a famous writer and actually mean it."
"Arakawa-san..."
"...Well, feel free to contact me if that day ever arrives.@k@vS10/62/410101742. Although, the pay isn't great and the job demands a lot of running around."
"...Got it!"
"But I'll have to give up on this story."

"Between the yakuza power struggle and the dead crying and apologizing to the living, the Weekly Seven would never publish this... My head is starting to hurt just thinking about what to do with this."
"Ahahaha..."
Otobe quietly chuckled.
Huh... so he can smile like this...
Otobe had a bright, youthful smile on his face.
Then, Towada-san bowed her head to us... before heading out to see Miyuki-chan.
After watching her confidently walk off, the rest of us turned to one another... and quietly nodded.
"You guys are a long way from town.@k@vS10/44/410500058. I'll drop you off there."
Along with Shion-san's voice, I heard the sound of a car horn.
A black car had pulled up right behind us without me even realizing it.
Shion-san got out of there, and a man whose appearance betrayed his aura walked over to us as if guiding her.
...Sensing his power, I braced myself for a moment.
"Oh, this is Kasai.@k@vS10/44/410500060. Don't worry about him."
"Y-yes, ma'am..."
I couldn't help but give an overly formal response. ...What am I doing?
Then, we climbed into the elegant car and Kasai-san drove us down a straight road leading to the village.
Otobe quickly fell asleep. He must've felt relieved.
I idly gazed out the window.
Then... at the foot of the rough mountains, a massive pile of rubble came into view.
"What is that...?@k@vS10/62/410101747. It looks like the ruins of some collapsed building...?"
"It's the remains of a school.@k@vS10/44/410500062. It's been left untouched since the explosion during the hostage incident.@k@vS10/44/410500063. ...Kasai, could you stop here?"
Kasai-san seemed to completely understand. He parked the car along the side of the ruins, then opened the rear passenger door.
Shion-san slowly emerged from the car... and let out a deep sigh.

"This is where everyone died...@k@vS10/44/410500065. I lost my sister, my friends, and everything in that incident over 20 years ago.@k@vS10/44/410500066. The only survivors were myself and the boy Sis liked.@k@vS10/44/410500067. ...But even he died shortly afterwards."
"That's why I thought...@k@vS10/44/410500069. I should've died with them in the explosion that day.@k@vS10/44/410500070. All this time, I thought I was left alone.@k@vS10/44/410500071. ...Always."
"Shion-san..."
Shion-san forlornly gazed on as the wind blew through the mountain of rubble.
Has she been tormented for the last 20 years by the lonely thought that she was the one left behind...?
She abandoned the name Shion and lived her life as Mion Sonozaki to protect the Sonozaki family.
Enduring that for all this time... must've been so painful.
"......Your sister once told us,@k@vS10/62/410101751. "Life is like a tube of toothpaste.@k@vS10/62/410101752. You have to squeeze every last drop out of it.""
"Sis said that...?"
"Ahahahahahaha, that sounds like something she'd say!"
Otobe came crawling out of the car. Shion-san's booming laughter must've woken him up.
Shion-san seemed lost in thought while gazing at his face... then, once he came closer she patted him on the head.
"...Eh? Shion-san?"
"Live your life unwaveringly!@k@vS10/44/410500075. I'll do the same,@k@vS10/44/410500076. even if every day is full of blood and gunpowder! Hehehe."
Otobe suddenly seemed to be embarrassed about a woman stroking his head. His face turned red.
When Shion-san saw that, she quietly muttered to herself.
"You look so much like Satoshi-kun..."
"Huh?"
"...It's nothing."
Shion-san turned away from us and gazed at the school while playing with her hair in her fingertips.
"For a moment there, you reminded me of an old friend.@k@vS10/44/410500080. There's a bit of a resemblance.@k@vS10/44/410500081. How would I describe him? ...Yeah, he was a good guy."
"So, did this Satoshi guy also pass away in this inci...?"
"Alright, that's enough idle chatter."
"With this bell, I can end this conflict in an instant.@k@vS10/44/410500084. And I need to settle the score with those Oumi punks who tried to take advantage of all this chaos."
Although it may still be a while longer until my work is complete...
Shion-san whispered that, then turned back this way... The face of the person who had muttered "Satoshi-kun" had completely vanished.
"Ah, Shion-san..."
"What is it?@k@vS10/44/410500086. If you take too long, I'll leave you behind."
"...Shion-san, there's something I absolutely must tell you here.@k@vS10/41/410201020. ...Before leaving Hinamizawa."
"The very last thing Mion-san said to me.@k@vS10/41/410201022. She wanted you to hear this, Shion-san..."
Shion-san stopped in her tracks.
"I have... one more favor to ask..."
I recalled the moment just before Mion Sonozaki's death, when she requested something of Otobe.
Come to think of it... what did she ask him?
"Mion-san said... "I hope... we can be twins again next time..."@k@vS10/41/410201024. That's what she wanted you to hear."
"Hang in there... Mion.@k@vS10/44/410500090. I'll do my best as Shion."

"Do your best, Mion.@k@vS10/44/410500092. If you run into any trouble, feel free to consult with me any time.@k@vS10/44/410500093. That's how it's always been for us.@k@vS10/44/410500094. And that'll never change."
Sheesh... that's seriously what you chose to say at the bitter end?
Even though she helped out back then, she's always butting in where she doesn't belong... treating me like a child.
I'm an adult now, you know? Much older than you were. Seriously... I can't believe your last words were something so naive...
And the word "next"... made me sad. Because you reminded me that you're already gone.
I wasn't interested in reminiscing about the old days. ...I wanted to talk about those memories with you.
I wanted to keep you by my side forever... "Sis"...!!
Tears started to well up in Shion-san's eyes.
"Shion-san..."
"Ahaha, ahahahahahah!@k@vS10/44/410500097. Whaaat? How could she say something so obvious?!"
Shion-san pulled the Tamahajiki sword from its sheath and thrust the blade into the ground near the former school building.
"Thanks, Sis...!"
Then... Shion-san turned and didn't look back.
Somewhere among the ruins, a single playing card fluttered in the wind.
That card probably dated back to the days when Mion Sonozaki played at this school.
The playing card gently fell to the ground, nestled next to the sword.
"...Thanks, Sonozaki-san.@k@vS10/62/410101757. And farewell, Hinamizawa."
Then I, too, started walking without looking back.
All the while, swearing in my heart that I'd never look back on the past.
Otobe and Towada-san had already started walking forward.
The car Kasai-san drove us here in slowly descended the mountain path.
Everyone here started walking toward their own separate future.
All of us starting at a disadvantage.
Otobe's future involved repaying a debt he could never pay off alone.
Towada-san's future included being arrested and sent to prison.
Sonozaki-san and Kasai-san's future involved fighting to protect the future of the Sonozaki Group.
And my future... is to start building something meaningful in my day to day life, while panicking about the uncertainty of tomorrow.
None of us will have it easy in the future.
But regardless, we'll keep walking forward. Fighting alone against our fate, for the right to say we "lived."
Mion Sonozaki... and... Hinamizawa village...
Farewell.
And... thank you.
A refreshing blue sky was spread out beyond the mountains, shining the light of hope down on our future.
